qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Embedded guest chart-data authorization allows saved-chart filter tampering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the embedded guest chart-data authorization path in POST /api/v1/chart/data, while the advisory describes a SQLLab PostgreSQL read-only verification bypass that misses crafted DML statements on database connections.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The accepted session finding is an embedded guest filter-tampering bypass in chart-data authorization, not the advisory's PostgreSQL read-only SQL validation bypass in SQLLab.
evidence: The advisory summary states that authenticated SQLLab users can bypass the read-only verification check on PostgreSQL connections with specially crafted SQL statements that evade DML detection.
The manifest at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_150944cf/.judge/input_manifest.json lists exactly one final qualifying finding: embedded_guest_chart_filter_tampering.

That finding's report and PoC describe tampering with adhoc_filters and queries[].filters on POST /api/v1/chart/data using a guest token to retrieve different chart data, which is a different endpoint, trust boundary, and root-cause mechanism from SQLLab read-only PostgreSQL validation.
